name: Tomah Area
description_short: Tomah Area Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union serving 6,720 members across 2 Wisconsin locations with $116 million in assets.
description_long:
Tomah Area Credit Union was established in 1939 and has operated for 87 years as a member-owned financial cooperative. Headquartered in Tomah, Wisconsin, the institution is federally insured and state-chartered under charter number 66615, with a main office at 940 N Superior Ave and one additional branch location. The credit union maintains $116.12 million in total assets and serves approximately 6,720 members with a staff of 23 full and part-time employees.

The credit union offers a range of consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and lending products. Their loan portfolio includes real estate loans ($46.6 million), unsecured personal loans ($1.1 million), and other loan products ($12.4 million). The organization provides first-time homebuyer programs and low-cost wire transfer services, positioning itself as a community-focused lender. As an FHLB (Federal Home Loan Bank) member, Tomah Area maintains access to wholesale funding and liquidity resources.

Tomah Area Credit Union distinguishes itself through deep community roots spanning nearly nine decades and a membership-based structure that prioritizes member interests over shareholder profits. The credit union reported a net income of $1.5 million in its most recent financial period and maintains a strong equity position of $14.3 million. Their low employee-to-member ratio and local decision-making model contrast with larger regional and national institutions.

As a mid-sized credit union, Tomah Area offers limited geographic reach (2 branches in Wisconsin) and smaller service capacity compared to larger financial institutions. Members seeking specialized products, extensive branch networks, or advanced digital banking platforms may find options elsewhere. The organization serves its core membership base effectively but operates within the typical constraints of regional credit unions regarding technology infrastructure and product breadth. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificate of deposit products (implied through deposit categories)
NCUSIF federal deposit insurance (up to $250,000 per account category)
pros: Federally insured state-chartered credit union with 87-year history and established community presence
$116.12 million in assets demonstrates financial stability and sustainability
Offers first-time homebuyer programs specifically designed for new homebuyers
Low-cost wire transfer services reduce consumer fees for money transfers
Member-based cooperative structure means profits benefit members rather than outside shareholders
Maintains FHLB membership providing access to competitive wholesale funding
Strong capital position with $14.3 million in equity relative to loan portfolio
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only 2 branch locations, restricting convenience for non-local members
No mention of digital banking capabilities, mobile app, or online-only account opening in available materials
Smaller institution with fewer specialized products compared to major regional credit unions
Limited information about loan rates, APRs, or fee schedules publicly available on listed website
Membership eligibility may be restricted based on geographic location or employer affiliation not specified in profile
best_for: Wisconsin residents in the Tomah area seeking local relationship banking and community-focused financial services
First-time homebuyers looking for specialized mortgage programs and local lending support
Members who value member-owned cooperative structures and community reinvestment over corporate banking
Savers and borrowers prioritizing personalized service and lower fees through a small regional institution
diagnosis: Tomah Area Credit Union is best suited for Wisconsin residents in the Tomah area who value community banking relationships, member-ownership benefits, and personalized service over extensive branch networks or advanced digital platforms. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—with only 2 branch locations, the credit union serves primarily local members; those requiring multi-state access or digital-first banking may need alternative institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
